items.title: Trump unveils historic US$500bil defence boost, targets 10pct cuts to other federal programmes
items.summary: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump on Friday requested a 10 per cent cut in non-defence spending for the 2027 fiscal year and a massive US$500 billion increase in the military budget, as the US continues its war against Iran. The request ultimately requires approval by Congress, where disagreement over Trump's spending decisions recently led to the longest government shutdown in US history. If enacted, total federal spending would reach US$2.2 trillion in 2027, compared with the roughly US$1.8 trillion spent for the current fiscal year.

items.title: Pope leads torch-lit Colosseum procession before Easter
items.summary: ROME: Pope Leo XIV led a torch-lit procession at the Colosseum in Rome on Friday as he prepares for his first Easter as pontiff in the shadow of war in the Middle East. Among the 30,000 people attending the poignant ceremony, known as the Way of the Cross, was Sarah, a Palestinian Catholic. "The pope is the only purely selfless political leader...

items.title: Loke urges public transport users to stay vigilant after LRT track incident
items.summary: He issued the reminder following an incident in which a passenger fell onto the tracks of the Kelana Jaya Light Rail Transit (LRT) line at the Abdullah Hukum Station yesterday. "I was informed of an incident involving a passenger who fell onto the LRT tracks at the Abdullah Hukum Station last evening, which led to the activation of the Platform Intrusion Emergency Stop (PIEC) safety system as a train approached the station. Earlier, Rapid Rail Sdn Bhd (Rapid Rail) said the Kelana Jaya Line LRT service, which was disrupted at 6.09pm after a passenger fell on the tracks at the Abdullah Hukum Station, was restored at 6.59pm.

items.title: Four houses, motorcycle and Hilux destroyed in Kota Baru fire
items.summary: The Kelantan Fire and Rescue Department's Operations Control Centre said it received an emergency call at 9.55pm, before firemen arrived at the scene 15 minutes later. "A total of 34 personnel, including from the Volunteer Fire Brigade (PBS), were deployed to the scene involving the Kota Baru, Kota Darul Naim, Wakaf Baru and Pengkalan Chepa fire and rescue stations, and the fire was brought under control at 11.03pm," the statement said. Meanwhile, Kota Baru Fire and Rescue Station chief Zuzuki Safei, when contacted by Bernama, said the fire did not involve any casualties or loss of life.— BERNAMA © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Malaysia has severe punishments for intoxicated drivers, but enforcement gaps remain
items.summary: Driving under the influence of alcohol or drugs is not an "accident". From January 2021 to September 2024 alone, 149 drink driving accident cases involving injuries and deaths were heard in court under Section 44 of the Road Transport Act 1987. THE LAW IS TOUGH — ON PAPER Malaysia's laws on drink driving have been significantly tightened in recent years.

items.title: When one Malayan tapir dies, what else is lost?
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 4 — The Malayan tapir is one of Malaysia’s most recognisable forest animals — and one of its most vulnerable. In recent years, tapir roadkill has emerged as a recurring issue across multiple states, particularly in areas where highways cut through or sit near forest reserves. Under the Wildlife Conservation Act 2010 [Act 716], there are no specific provisions for accidental killing of protected wildlife by vehicles; mandatory wildlife crossings such as eco-viaducts or underpasses; enforceable speed limits in wildlife corridors; and required mitigation after repeated roadkill incidents.

items.title: Iran downs US F-15 as crews scramble in high-stakes recovery race
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iranian and American forces were racing each other early Saturday to recover the crew of the first US fighter jet to go down inside Iran since the start of the war. Teheran said it had shot down the F-15 warplane, while US media reported American special forces had rescued one of two crew members. Iran's military also said it downed a US A-10 ground attack aircraft in the Gulf, with US media saying the pilot was rescued.

items.title: Geopolitical conflict exposes Malaysian healthcare vulnerability
items.summary: Malaysia talks endlessly about the cost of healthcare. What the Iran, United States and Israel conflict may yet expose is something more uncomfortable — the vulnerability of healthcare itself. That is why this conflict should concern Malaysian healthcare.

items.title: Man detained over alleged kitten abuse in Kajang laundrette
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Selangor Department of Veterinary Services (DVS) has detained a man suspected of abusing a kitten in an incident at a self-service laundrette in the Kajang area near here. Selangor DVS said in a statement that the man, believed to be in his 40s, was detained after the department was deployed to the scene at about 11am today to conduct an inspection. The statement said the action followed information and public complaints after a 59-second video went viral on social media, showing a man abusing a kitten by repeatedly throwing it against a wall and onto a mattress at a self-service laundrette.

items.title: PM: Boost silat to build youth with character and spirit
items.summary: KUANTAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim wants the martial art of silat to be strengthened, especially among young people, as it not only builds character and self-discipline but also instils a spirit of struggle. "This spirit must be cultivated. At the event, Anwar awarded former national silat athlete Fazly Mansoh the Pahang Madani Pendekar Award, and former national athlete Suzy Mohd Sulaiman the Madani Srikandi Award.

items.title: 'Do not abuse power': Fahmi Fadzil issues stern warning to PKR leaders
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: PKR information chief Datuk Fahmi Fadzil has reminded party members not to abuse their positions, warning that misconduct will not be tolerated under the current administration. Speaking at the Federal Territories PKR general assembly meeting launch today, he said those entrusted with responsibility must not take advantage of their positions or engage in actions that could tarnish the party's image. Good luck to you," he said.

items.title: Fahmi Fadzil's defamation hearing against trio begins Monday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil's defamation suit against three people over allegations of giving a political speech inside a mosque in 2023 will begin on Monday. He said the case will be heard at the High Court here, naming Wan Azri Wan Deris, Ahmad Dusuki Abd Rani and Mohd Fauzan Madzlan as defendants. "God willing, on Monday, I will begin my defamation suit against three individuals who slandered me in 2023," he said, speaking to reporters after launching the FT PKR general meeting for FT PKR branches.

items.title: Three tankers, including one Japanese-owned, cross Strait of Hormuz near Oman
items.summary: LONDON: Three tankers, including one co-owned by a Japanese company, crossed the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday by hugging close to Oman's shore, a rare transit route as Iran maintains a chokehold on the key war-torn passageway. The Sohar LNG is the first LNG tanker to have crossed the strait since March 1, when Iranian attacks against ships near the Strait of Hormuz, in retaliation for US-Israel strikes, reduced crossings to a near stop. The Sohar LNG vessel is jointly owned by Japanese shipping company Mitsui O.S.K, according to Japanese media reports quoting a company statement on Friday.

items.title: MDEC, Jakim to collaborate on AI-based learning at selected pilot institutions
items.summary: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the initiative will be implemented at higher education and Islamic institutions in Kedah, Kelantan, Negri Sembilan, Pahang, Perak and Penang, benefiting about 3,000 students. "All students should be given greater exposure to AI, including those in higher education and Islamic institutions," he said when addressing the Himpunan Anak Pahang Madani at the UTC Kuantan grounds here today. Anwar, who is also the finance minister, said the initiative will equip students with greater exposure to AI technology while fostering critical thinking and digital innovation.

items.title: MCMC takes down 159 false posts linked to global crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has taken down 159 pieces of false social media content over a four-day period linked to misinformation exploiting the ongoing global crisis. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the content fell into three main categories. "First, false content related to fuel price hikes.

items.title: Global energy crisis: MCMC investigates 17 people for spreading fake info
items.summary: KULAI: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) is investigating 17 individuals so far believed to have spread fake information on social media about the global energy crisis. Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said the individuals included those identified to have edited pictures and infographics displaying fake information using the names of local news agencies. – Bernama TAGS: MCMC, investigation, Deputy Communications Minister, energy crisis © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: NST Leader: High time for a National Harmony Act?
items.summary: Race relations in Malaysia, despite the occasional flare-ups, are well under control. In previous years, police quelled racial tensions by wielding the Internal Security Act. Since its abolishment, the replacement Security Offences (Special Measures) Act 2012 has been used on 6,900 people until last year, mostly for human trafficking, migrant smuggling, organised crime and terror-related offences.

items.title: Education, morals, human values key to shaping future generation - PM
items.summary: KUANTAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim stressed that holistic education, combined with strong moral grounding and human values, is a crucial foundation to shaping the country's future generation. He said this at the 'Anak Pahang Madani' Gathering here today, which was also attended by Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail. Also present were Youth and Sports Minister Dr Mohammed Taufiq Johari, the Prime Minister's political secretary, Datuk Ahmad Farhan Fauzi; and Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar.

items.title: Immigration cracks down on 'Geng Ashraf' migrant smuggling ring
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Immigration Department has successfully busted a migrant smuggling ring known as "Geng Ashraf", which is believed to have earned approximately RM2.4 million since it began operating in January 2025. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the syndicate smuggled foreign nationals through third countries into Malaysia via Kelantan, and then transported them to the Klang Valley for distribution to various destinations. He emphasised that the Immigration Department will take strict action against anyone found to have violated the Immigration Act 1959/63, Passport Act 1966, Immigration Regulations 1963, or Anti-Trafficking in Persons and Anti-Smuggling of Migrants Act.

items.title: Call for lifetime bans, stricter laws in fatal DUI cases
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Parti Pejuang Tanah Air (Pejuang) has urged the government to take stricter action against fatal road offences, including introducing strict liability laws in cases involving driving under the influence (DUI). She said the government must move beyond a reactive approach, stressing that firm action, clear policies and effective enforcement must be implemented without delay. She called for the enforcement of strict liability laws in fatal accidents involving alcohol, where intoxicated drivers would be held directly accountable without the need for lengthy proof of negligence.

items.title: RM2.8b energy incentives benefit 9.2 million consumers in Peninsular Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: About 9.2 million electricity consumers in Peninsular Malaysia have benefited from the Energy Efficiency Incentive, with total savings amounting to RM2.8 billion since its introduction, aimed at easing the financial burden amid the global energy crisis. "Consumers with monthly electricity usage of 1,000kWh and below are eligible for the Energy Efficiency Incentive, which encourages more efficient energy consumption," she said in a statement today. Siti Safinah also urged the public to adopt prudent energy usage to ensure long-term sustainability.

items.title: EG Industries raises stake in listed Thai firm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: EG Industries Bhd has increased its stake in Thailand-listed ND Rubber Public Co Ltd to 41.59 per cent, strengthening its position in energy and electric vehicle (EV)-related solutions. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, EG Industries said it had completed the acquisition of an additional 80 million shares in ND Rubber via a voluntary partial tender offer at 1.50 baht per share on March 27, 2026. EG Industries said the offer price represents a premium of about 31.6 per cent over ND Rubber's 15-day volume weighted average price and 32.7 per cent over its 90-day average, which the board considers fair and reasonable.

items.title: MACC detains two over RM1mil bribery case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has detained two individuals over their alleged involvement in a bribery case worth about RM1 million. "The main suspect was detained in a special operation conducted by the MACC Intelligence Division at a restaurant while receiving three cheques worth about RM1 million," he said. He said that during the arrest, MACC also seized several items, including the three cheques and four mobile phones.

items.title: Today's news in pictures – Friday, April 3, 2026
items.summary: MISSED what happened today? Here's a glimpse at what happened throughout the country in photographs. [[nid:1409788]] [[nid:1409787]] [[nid:1409786]] [[nid:1409785]] [[nid:1409784]] [[nid:1409783]] © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Anwar, AIIB president discuss sustainable development agenda
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and the president and chair of the Board of Directors of the Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank (AIIB), Zou Jiayi, have discussed a sustainable development agenda covering the transition to green energy as well as the empowerment of digital infrastructure and advanced technology. "I emphasised the government's priorities as outlined in the National Energy Transition Roadmap (NETR) and the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP), which, among other things, focus on the empowerment of renewable energy, energy efficiency improvements, and the development of a competitive and inclusive digital ecosystem," he said in a social media post. Anwar, who is also the finance min, said he received Zou's courtesy visit on Thursday.

items.title: News@9: Today's top headlines - April 3, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: WFH not for leisure Chief Secretary Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar reminded civil servants that working from home must follow rules and maintain productivity. DoE chief claims trial Environment Department director-general Wan Abdul Latiff Wan Jaffar pleaded not guilty to accepting a RM100,000 bribe linked to an environmental approval in Penang. Segamat crash driver on drugs A lorry driver involved in a fatal crash that killed three people in Segamat tested positive for methamphetamine.

items.title: Company manager loses over RM1.3mil in fraudulent land sale scheme
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A company manager lost more than RM1.3 million after allegedly falling victim to a fraudulent land sale scheme in Kulai earlier this year. Kulai police chief assistant commissioner Tan Seng Lee said a report on the incident was lodged on Jan 8 by a 42-year-old man. Initial investigations found that the victim's company, through its appointed lawyer, had expressed interest in purchasing a property and subsequently paid a deposit to a law firm purportedly representing the seller.

items.title: Man loses RM1.3m after ‘law firm’ allegedly releases deposit in fake land deal
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 3 — A company manager lost more than RM1.3 million after allegedly falling victim to a fraudulent land sale scheme in Kulai earlier this year. Kulai police chief ACP Tan Seng Lee said a report on the incident was lodged on January 8 by a 42-year-old man. Initial investigations found that the victim’s company, through its appointed lawyer, had expressed interest in purchasing a property and subsequently paid a deposit to a law firm purportedly representing the seller.

items.title: Italy sees EU easing deficit rules if Middle East crisis lasts
items.summary: ROME: The European Union will inevitably consider easing its budget deficit rules if the US-Israeli war against Iran continues to drive up energy costs for families and businesses, Italy's economy minister said on Friday. "It is clear that, unless the situation changes, discussions at the European level will be inevitable," Giorgetti said when asked whether Italy would urge the EU to relax its rules limiting budget deficits to three per cent of national output. His remarks suggest Italy may fail to bring its deficit down to 2.8 per cent of gross domestic product this year from 3.1 per cent in 2025 as planned, due to lower-than-expected growth amid rising energy bills.

items.title: Police arrest 67 in raids on 13 illegal gambling dens across Selangor and Sabah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — Police arrested 67 individuals in raids conducted at 13 premises suspected of conducting illegal gambling in Selangor and Sabah from Wednesday until yesterday. “The 13 premises include eight in Seri Kembangan and North Klang, Selangor and five premises in Kota Kinabalu, Tawau and Sandakan, Sabah,” he said in a statement here today, adding that various electronic equipment was seized, including nine televisions, tablets (95), laptops (17), controllers/consoles (61), simulator machines (20), computers (38) and RM16,553 in cash. “The suspects are being investigated under Section 4B(a), 4(1)(c) and 6(1) of the Common Gaming Houses Act 1953 as well as Section 6(1)(c) and 15(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63, he added.

items.title: ‘What is the meaning of AI if humanity is eroded?’ Anwar tells youths at Pahang gathering
items.summary: KUANTAN, April 3 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim stressed that holistic education, combined with strong moral grounding and human values, is a crucial foundation to shaping the country’s future generation. He said this at the ‘Anak Pahang Madani’ gathering here today, which was also attended by Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail. Also present were Youth and Sports Minister Dr Mohammed Taufiq Johari; the Prime Minister’s political secretary, Datuk Ahmad Farhan Fauzi; and Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar.

items.title: MDEC, Jakim to pilot AI learning facilities across selected institutions nationwide, says Anwar
items.summary: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the initiative will be implemented at higher education and Islamic institutions in Kedah, Kelantan, Negeri Sembilan, Pahang, Perak and Penang, benefiting about 3,000 students. “All students should be given greater exposure to AI, including those in higher education and Islamic institutions,” he said when addressing the Himpunan Anak Pahang Madani at the Kuantan UTC grounds here today. Anwar, who is also the finance minister, said the initiative will equip students with greater exposure to AI technology while fostering critical thinking and digital innovation.

items.title: Woman trapped under train at Abdullah Hukum station [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A woman survived a harrowing ordeal after she became pinned beneath a train when she fell onto the tracks at Abdullah Hukum LRT station in Bangsar today. The Kuala Lumpur Fire and Rescue Department said a team from the Pantai fire station was despatched after receiving a distress call at 6.19pm. Operations commander Wan Mohd Shahrir Azizi Wan Said said the victim was found pinned under the train upon the team's arrival.

items.title: Wisma Putra: Malaysia condemns Israeli law mandating death penalty for Palestinians
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 3 — Malaysia strongly condemns a new law passed by the Zionist Israeli regime imposing a mandatory death sentence on Palestinians in the Occupied Palestinian Territory. “Such legislation, which targets individuals based on ethnicity and national identity, constitutes a clear violation of international humanitarian law and international human rights law, including the Fourth Geneva Convention and relevant international human rights instruments,” the statement said. The ministry noted that the new law represents yet another discriminatory measure by the Zionist Israeli regime to further entrench its apartheid system under its illegal occupation of Palestinian territory.

items.title: Malaysia’s healthcare reform drive calls for stronger private sector role, says Dzulkefly
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 3 — Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad today outlined five key strategic priorities requiring strong support from the private sector as part of efforts to accelerate the transformation of Malaysia’s healthcare system into a more sustainable, integrated and patient-centred model. He said the priorities include active participation in the national healthcare plan, early involvement in pilot initiatives, a transition towards value-based care, enhanced data-sharing collaboration for fairer cost structures, and stronger integration of interoperable digital health systems with public platforms such as MySejahtera. “The public healthcare sector will always remain the backbone of the nation.

items.title: PTT Synergy bags RM31.68mil job from SD Property
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: PTT Synergy Group Bhd has secured a RM31.68 million contract for earthworks and ancillary works at Bandar Bukit Raja in Klang, Selangor. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia on Friday, PTT Synergy said its wholly-owned Pembinaan Tetap Teguh Sdn Bhd had accepted a letter of award from Sime Darby Property (Bukit Raja) Sdn Bhd for the project. PTT Synergy said pre-possession of the site is scheduled for April 3, 2026, with possession commencing on April 17.

items.title: Christians observe Good Friday with solemn services
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Christians marked Good Friday with solemn services today, commemorating the crucifixion and death of Jesus Christ. Observed during Holy Week, Good Friday is part of the Easter Triduum, culminating in Easter Sunday, which celebrates Christ's resurrection. Priest Father Alvin Ho Sj of St. Francis Xavier's Church said the day centres on the Passion of Christ and His sacrifice for humanity.

items.title: Malaysia’s weather does a 180: Bye heat, hello afternoon storms
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 3 — Malaysia is now in the monsoon transition phase, from March 29 until May, with heavy rain and strong winds expected over short periods, thus bringing relief from the hot weather phenomenon that has hit the country of late, said Minister of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup. He said the current hot weather phenomenon is an annual occurrence that usually begins towards the end of the Northeast Monsoon season until June, due to reduced cloud cover, particularly in the northern part of Peninsular Malaysia. "The arrival of the monsoon transition phase is expected to bring more frequent heavy rain and strong winds over short periods, particularly in the afternoon to early evening.

items.title: Passenger incident at Abdullah Hukum LRT station disrupts Kelana Jaya line service during rush hour
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — Train services on the Kelana Jaya Line were disrupted this evening following a passenger incident at the Abdullah Hukum LRT station, triggering an emergency shutdown and rescue operation during the evening peak. Rail operator Rapid Rail Sdn Bhd said the incident occurred at 6.09pm, confirming that “an incident occurred involving a passenger at Abdullah Hukum Station”. “Alternative transport services, including free shuttle bus services, have been activated to support operations,” Rapid Rail said.

items.title: Contractor under scrutiny after second pipe failure floods Seremban Main Market
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 3 — The Seremban City Council (MBS) will summon the contractor responsible for managing the Seremban Main Market building following an incident in which the second floor was flooded due to a burst water tank pipe early today. MBS Mayor Datuk Masri Baharuddin said the council was informed that damage had previously occurred at the same location, leading to the pipe bursting again. He stressed that the council would not compromise and would blacklist the contractor if found to be negligent.

items.title: RM2.8b in savings: Energy incentives cushion 9.2 million users from fuel price swings in Peninsular Malaysia, says Energy Commission
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — About 9.2 million electricity consumers in Peninsular Malaysia have benefited from the Energy Efficiency Incentive, with total savings amounting to RM2.8 billion since its introduction, aimed at easing the financial burden amid the global energy crisis. “Consumers with monthly electricity usage of 1,000 kWh and below are eligible for the Energy Efficiency Incentive, which encourages more efficient energy consumption,” she said in a statement today. Siti Safinah also urged the public to adopt prudent energy usage to ensure long-term sustainability.

items.title: Immigration busts ‘Geng Ashraf’ smuggling ring in Ampang, linked to RM2.4m in profits
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 3 — The Malaysian Immigration Department has successfully busted a migrant smuggling ring known as “Geng Ashraf”, which is believed to have earned approximately RM2.4 million since it began operating in January 2025. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the syndicate smuggled foreign nationals through third countries into Malaysia via Kelantan, and then transported them to the Klang Valley for distribution to various destinations. He emphasised that the Immigration Department will take strict action against anyone found to have violated the Immigration Act 1959/63, Passport Act 1966, Immigration Regulations 1963, or Act 670.

items.title: Two teenagers killed, one injured after Perodua Ativa skids on Johor highway
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 3 — Two teenagers were killed and another seriously injured after the Perodua Ativa they were in skidded and hit a concrete embankment along Kilometre 14 of Lebuhraya Iskandar Sultan early this morning. North Johor Bahru district police chief ACP Radin Ramlan Radin Taha said they received word of the crash that killed both the car driver, a boy, 19, and the front passenger, a girl, 16, at about 4.25am. “The car was on its way from Johor Bahru to Tampol when it lost control and skidded and hit the concrete embankment on the left side of the road,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Anwar meets AIIB chief to discuss Malaysia’s green energy and digital infrastructure plans
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and the president and chair of the Board of Directors of the Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank (AIIB), Zou Jiayi, have discussed a sustainable development agenda covering the transition to green energy as well as the empowerment of digital infrastructure and advanced technology. “I emphasised the government’s priorities as outlined in the National Energy Transition Roadmap (NETR) and the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP), which, among other things, focus on the empowerment of renewable energy, energy efficiency improvements, and the development of a competitive and inclusive digital ecosystem,” he said in a social media post. Anwar, who is also the minister of finance, said he received Zou’s courtesy visit on Thursday.

items.title: Selangor MB to lead Malaysia’s Gaza aid mission, joins Global Sumud Flotilla 2.0 push
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 3 — Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari, on behalf of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, will lead Malaysia’s 30-Container Humanitarian Mission to Gaza, as well as the Global Sumud Flotilla 2.0 delegation. The Selangor Menteri Besar’s Office said the mission, organised by the Malaysian Consultative Council of Islamic Organisations (MAPIM), will deliver 30 containers carrying 374 tonnes of aid to Palestinians in Gaza, where conditions are increasingly dire. “The Menteri Besar will depart on April 5 to lead the humanitarian delegation and ensure the containers are safely cleared in Cairo.

items.title: Penang MACC arrests two over RM1m bribe to suppress corruption report against sand supplier
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has arrested a managing director and a jobless man for allegedly soliciting a RM1 million bribe to bury a corruption report against a sand supplier. One was arrested at a restaurant, while the other was detained at the MACC Penang office. MACC Deputy Chief Commissioner (Operations) Datuk Seri Ahmad Khusairi Yahaya confirmed the arrests when contacted.

items.title: Jalan Sultan Ismail red light crash: Man in KL charged with dangerous driving after crashing into two bikers, causing one to lose his leg
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — A man featured in a viral video of a car crashing into two motorcyclists yesterday pleaded not guilty today at the Magistrate’s Court to two charges of reckless and dangerous driving. T S Kugenthiran, 25, is accused of having driven recklessly, endangering Muhammad Afiq Zayani Azliyuszaini, 24, and Muhammad Hairy Abdul Rahman, 19, on Jalan Sultan Ismail, heading from the city centre towards Tuanku Abdul Rahman, at 4.30 am on April 2. Screenshot from a CCTV footage circulating on social media of a red car (left) that ran a red light on Jalan Sultan Ismail towards Tuanku Abdul Rahman, Kuala Lumpur moments before impact with two motorcyclists on April 2, 2026.

items.title: TikToker sues Malaysian Hindu Sangam president for defamation after video linking her flower bathing service to fraud
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — A TikTok influencer has filed a defamation suit against Malaysian Hindu Sangam president T. Ganesan over a video allegedly linking her flower bathing service to deceptive practices. Entrepreneur Olivia Lopez Bennet Lopez, 45, better known as Ibu Olivia Lopez, filed the suit at the Shah Alam Sessions Court last March through Messrs Asheeq Ali & Co, naming Ganesan as the defendant. In her statement of claim, Olivia Lopez, who operates a flower bathing service, alleged that the defendant posted a Facebook Reel in August last year containing defamatory remarks about the services she offers.

items.title: Socso to provide RM2,888 monthly lifeline to Klang rider’s widow and three kids
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 3 — The welfare of the widow and three children of a motorcyclist, killed in a road accident at Jalan Raya Barat, Klang, last Sunday, will continue to be safeguarded, as they qualify for a monthly Survivors’ Pension of RM2,888.17 from the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso). Perkeso Group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed said checks found that the late Amirul Hafiz Omar, 33, who had worked at TVH Malaysia Sdn Bhd for 15 years, was an active contributor under the Employees’ Social Security Act 1969 (Act 4). “This new Act officially provides social security protection under Perkeso to more than 1.64 million gig workers in Malaysia, ensuring compensation and welfare if they are involved in accidents while working,” he said.

items.title: After demanding a ‘life for a life’, Klang rider’s widow seeks tougher laws to end drugged driving
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 3 — The widow of a man who died after being hit by a driver under the influence of drugs in Klang recently hopes the government will consider amending existing penalties and laws to prevent similar incidents from recurring. Nor Nadia Abdul Majid, 32, the wife of the late Amirul Hafiz Omar, 33, said she hopes her husband’s death would serve as a lesson to society and lead to changes in current legislation. “There may be wisdom behind my husband’s passing.

items.title: High-speed chase through Ipoh neighbourhood ends after suspected cable thieves in BMW hit roadside pole
items.summary: IPOH, April 3 — A high-speed pursuit through the streets of Bercham ended in a crash and the arrest of two men early this morning, with police discovering suspected drugs and cable theft equipment in their vehicle. The incident unfolded at approximately 2:30 AM when a mobile patrol vehicle unit on routine patrol spotted a vehicle behaving suspiciously. Ipoh district police chief ACP Najib Hamzah said the suspects drove dangerously before losing control and colliding with a roadside pole.

items.title: Hadi: New Opposition leader to be named before June Parliament meeting
items.summary: KUALA NERUS, 3 April — A new Opposition Leader will be finalised before the Dewan Rakyat reconvenes in June, PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said today. “This matter requires consensus because it involves all opposition MPs, not just a single party,” Hadi told reporters at an event here today. Under parliamentary convention, the Opposition leader is typically the head of the largest opposition bloc.

items.title: Could a Mediation Centre cut case backlogs and legal costs? Azalina says possible if more disputes stay outside court
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — The government is mulling the possibility of establishing a Malaysia Mediation Centre (MMC) under the Legal Aid Department to strengthen out-of-court dispute resolution, reduce case backlogs and lower legal costs for the public. Law and Institutional Reform Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department, Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said, said the initiative aims to streamline mediation systems currently administered separately by various ministries and agencies through their respective tribunals. Azalina noted that not all individuals can afford litigation or expensive legal advice, citing the high cost of legal letters and services.

items.title: Malaysia’s education initiatives bridge urban-rural gap in SPM 2025
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — The 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) results show the gap in National Average Grades (GPK) between urban and rural candidates narrowing, demonstrating the progress of Malaysia’s education democratisation initiatives. Director-general of Education Datuk Dr Mohd Azam Ahmad said the GPK for urban candidates was 4.40 in 2025, down from 4.47 in 2024, while the GPK for rural candidates fell to 4.81 last year from 4.95 the previous year. Meanwhile, Mohd Azam said the performance of candidates from Integrity Schools and Henry Gurney Schools recorded a 100 per cent pass rate in the 2025 SPM.

items.title: Hassan Marican leads Malaysia's new crisis task force to monitor fuel, food supply amid West Asia conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — Tan Sri Mohd Hassan Marican has been appointed as the chairman of the Crisis Management Task Force (PPPK) under the National Economic Action Council (MTEN) to coordinate the government’s efforts to address the global energy crisis following the conflict in West Asia. Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil said Hassan, who is also the head of the Special Advisory Body to the Minister of Finance, will be responsible for reviewing on a daily basis the actions that need to be taken, in addition to gathering feedback on West Asian issues. Fahmi also explained that the tasks of the team led by Hassan do not overlap with the special committee chaired by Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan, which is responsible for examining current economic developments following the conflict.

items.title: RTS Link on track for Jan 2027 opening; costs stable despite global energy crisis
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 3 — The global energy crisis triggered by the conflict in the Middle East has not impacted the progress of the Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System Link (RTS Link), Transport Minister Anthony Loke confirmed today. The RTS Link is entering a critical phase this year ahead of its launch in January 2027,” Loke told reporters following a briefing at the Bukit Chagar Customs, Immigration and Quarantine (CIQ) complex. Loke detailed the upcoming technical stages, which include system installation, acceptance testing, and dynamic testing.

items.title: RTS Link fare proposal to be presented by Q3, says Loke
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 3 — The proposed fare structure for the Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link is expected to be submitted for the Malaysian and Singaporean governments’ approval by the third quarter (Q3) of this year, said Transport Minister Anthony Loke. He said that the project’s joint operator, RTS Operations Pte Ltd, will present the proposed rates to both governments. “The Land Public Transport Agency in Malaysia and the Land Transport Authority in Singapore will need to approve the proposed fare rates before it is officially announced,” Loke told reporters after a project briefing at the CIQ complex here today.

items.title: Gig Workers Act 2025: Now in effect, HR Ministry rolls out tribunal and council to enforce protection
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — A total of 76 individuals were appointed today to the Gig Workers Tribunal (TPG) and the Gig Advisory Council (MPGig), in a move to strengthen the enforcement of the Gig Workers Act 2025, which came into effect on March 31. “The tribunal has jurisdiction to hear, mediate and adjudicate disputes between gig workers and contracting entities,” he told reporters after presenting appointment letters and certificates to TPG and MPGig members here today. Ramanan said key issues discussed at the council meeting today included a proposal to introduce a minimum earnings rate, or floor price, for gig workers, to be determined through a consensus between the government, workers and contracting entities.

items.title: DOE deputy director-general claims trial to taking RM21,000 bribes from waste company
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — Department of Environment (DOE) deputy director-general Dr Norhazni Mat Sari pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today, to six charges of receiving RM21,000 from a scheduled waste management company, four years ago. Deputy Public Prosecutor Datuk Ahmad Akram Gharib informed the court that the prosecution and defence had agreed to a bail of RM25,000 with one surety for all charges. “The prosecution is also requesting that the accused’s passport be surrendered to the court until the case is resolved,” said Ahmad Akram, who was also assisted by deputy public prosecutor Mohamad Fadhly Mohd Zamry.

items.title: An eight-year-old girl dies in Taiping, now her parents and stepparents will stand trial for murder
items.summary: TAIPING, April 3 — Four family members, including the biological mother, were charged in the Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of an eight-year-old girl last week. M. Savathri, 28, her husband M. Satis Kumar, 27, who is also the victim’s stepfather, the victim’s stepmother S.C. Veeraletchumy, 53, and stepfather M. Murali, 49, nodded after the charges were read out separately to them before Magistrate R. Prabakaran. However, no plea was recorded from any of the accused, as murder cases fall under the jurisdiction of the High Court.
